characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Alliterative Name:HenryHart. The Apprentice: Captain Man chooses Henry as his sidekick so he can take over for him one day. Badass Normal: Henry doesn't have superpowers like Ray does, and he even points this out during aGet a Hold of Yourself, Man!moment after Ray suffers fromHeroic BSoDaftertears from a Jolly Beetledisable his invulnerability.He is temporarily given super-fast reflexesbeforehaving to give up those powers to save the day in a later episode. Big Brother Instinct:In a literal sense towards Piper. Despite their bickering, hedoesshow that he cares about her. Most notably shown when she gets bullied by the President of the Captain Man fan club, when Dr. Minyak held her hostage at his house, and when Ray was about to wipe her memory.He is also shown to be very protective of Charlotte. In a literal sense towards Piper. Despite their bickering, hedoesshow that he cares about her. Most notably shown when she gets bullied by the President of the Captain Man fan club, when Dr. Minyak held her hostage at his house, and when Ray was about to wipe her memory.

characters.name: Henry Hart/Kid Danger
characters.description: Alliterative Name:HenryHart. The Apprentice: Captain Man chooses Henry as his sidekick so he can take over for him one day. Badass Normal: Henry doesn't have superpowers like Ray does, and he even points this out during aGet a Hold of Yourself, Man!moment after Ray suffers fromHeroic BSoDaftertears from a Jolly Beetledisable his invulnerability.He is temporarily given super-fast reflexesbeforehaving to give up those powers to save the day in a later episode. Big Brother Instinct:In a literal sense towards Piper. Despite their bickering, hedoesshow that he cares about her. Most notably shown when she gets bullied by the President of the Captain Man fan club, when Dr. Minyak held her hostage at his house, and when Ray was about to wipe her memory.He is also shown to be very protective of Charlotte. In a literal sense towards Piper. Despite their bickering, hedoesshow that he cares about her. Most notably shown when she gets bullied by the President of the Captain Man fan club, when Dr. Minyak held her hostage at his house, and when Ray was about to wipe her memory.

characters.name: Ray Manchester/Captain Man
characters.description: Big Brother Instinct: Mostly to Henry. He is also shown to be quite protective of Charlotte and Jasper. Big Brother Mentor: To Henry. Brought Down to Normal: Happens in "Tears of the Jolly Beetle" when he loses his invulnerability, he regains it at the end of the episode.He loses his powers permanently in the final episode. However, he later mentions that Schwoz will be able to get them back in a week or two. Bunny-Ears Lawyer: Eccentric, goofy & a bit dull, but one of the greatest superheroes around. Captain Superhero: Guess what the second word is.

characters.name: Charlotte Page
characters.description: Action Girl:Heavilyaverted. While she has shown interest in being Ray's sidekick, she later learns her skills are better suited for helping out behind the scenes. Affectionate Nickname: Often called "Char" by her friends. The Baby of the Bunch: While she is quite intelligent and can hold her own in an argument, she lacks in fighting skills. If anyone messes with her, Henry, Ray, Schwoz, and even Jasper don't hesitate to defend her. Berserk Button:Don'tEVERcall her Charlotte Sweb.Calling her a young woman or a girl sets her off. Don'tEVERcall her Charlotte Sweb.

characters.name: Jasper Dunlop
characters.description: Ascended Fanboy: He was a huge fan of Captain Man and Kid Danger before eventually being able to work with them in season 3, and still fanboyed over Captain Man after being hired by him. Big Brother Instinct: In "Brawl In the Hall" he wants to protect Charlotte, and tries to talk Bysh out of fighting her.In "Balloons of Doom", he also helps Captain Man and Kid Danger save Piper by putting up a damn good fight against Dr. Minyak (though, he does get defeated by him). In "Balloons of Doom", he also helps Captain Man and Kid Danger save Piper by putting up a damn good fight against Dr. Minyak (though, he does get defeated by him). Butt-Monkey: Sometimes, particularly around Piper. Cloudcuckoolander: Jasper comes off like this at times, though maybe not as much Sydney and Oliver.

characters.name: Piper Hart
characters.description: Annoying Younger Sibling: Frequently tries to get Henry in trouble with their parents. However, Henry doesn't treat her well either. Ascended Fanboy" Well, fangirl. She was a huge fan of Captain Man and Kid Danger before getting to work with them mid-season 5, and was even the president of their fan group Berserk Button:If anything bad happens to her phone, she willkillyou.Saying something negative to her really sets her off badly.Insult her cooking in any way or attempt to hide it while she is in the presence, and she will pull the table covers off your table, effectively cancelling dinner immediately.Don't ever ignore her or try to beat her at something she knows she will easily win at. Piper does not take loss very well and is only in it to win it! If anything bad happens to her phone, she willkillyou. Saying something negative to her really sets her off badly.

characters.name: Schwoz Schwartz
characters.description: Ray's friend who made most of the tech in the Man Cave. Alliterative Name:SchwozSchwartz. Amazon Chaser: If his ideal woman is any indication. Ascended Extra: He was a recurring character during the first four seasons, before being becoming a main character in the fifth and final season. Berserk Button: Unless you want some crap from Schwoz, don't ever interrupt or outsmart him bybeing snideful about something.

characters.name: Jake and Kris Hart
characters.description: Informed Attribute: Jake is shown to be a project manager, and mentioned to have been a Navy SEAL, yet he is usually shown as a helpless, immature buffoon who can arguably rival both Jasper and Ray. Pushover Parents: They usually fail to keep Piper in line. Too Dumb to Live: In the finale, Charlotte tasked Jake with an urgent, yet simple deed, which he botches anyway and sets the Man Cave to self-destruct.

characters.name: Sidney Birnbaum
characters.description: Ambiguously Jewish: "Birthday Girl Down" reveals that he celebrates Passover. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: He's never seen again after Season 3's "Live and Dangerous" (not counting his cameo appearance in Season 4's "Rubber Duck"). Those Two Guys: Is almost always seen with Oliver.

characters.name: Oliver Pook
characters.description: Asian and Nerdy: More nerdy than even Sidney. The Stoic: He speaks in a monotone voice, even in exciting situations. Those Two Guys: With Sidney, though he does appear in episodes without him unlike the other way around.

characters.name: Mr. Gooba Gooch
characters.description: Alliterative Name:Gooba'Gooch. Ambiguously Brown: His background is unknown, but his appearance and accent implies that he's of Indian origin. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Isn't seen after Season 1 (possibly due to hisstereotypical portrayal), with his fate unknown and Jasper took his place in season 3. Last-Name Basis: He is mostly referred to in his last name and his given name is rarely mentioned. Mission Control: His main role.

characters.name: Trent Overunder and Mary Gaperman
characters.description: Two wisecracking reporters for Swellview News. '20s Bob Haircut: Mary's usual hairstyle. The Dividual: They seem to function as a single unit and are never seen apart.

characters.name: Ms. Sharona Shapen
characters.description: Alliterative Name:SharonaShapen. The Dog Bites Back: She refuses to help Jasper up after getting bit by a poisonous spider that Henry brought for him at Pet-Me, most likely because he refused to help her up after she got hit by the motorcycle bandit a few days prior to that. Punny Name: Her name is a pun on "misshapen".

characters.name: Mitch Bilsky
characters.description: A local bully who has gotten held back in high school for over a decade. Ambiguous Situation: It is unknown if he was ever arrested after confessing to his framing of Jasper in "Mouth Candy".However, in "Dodging Danger" (four episodes later), he exclaims that "the Mitch is back!", implying that hedideventually get arrested for framing Jasper. However, in "Dodging Danger" (four episodes later), he exclaims that "the Mitch is back!", implying that hedideventually get arrested for framing Jasper. Even Evil Has Standards: Mitch may be a bully to everyone at Swellview High School on a regular basis, but even he has morals as shown in “Bilsky Billions”, where he was disgusted towards Jeff for attempting to get him in trouble. Expy: An unintelligent school bully who's older (and bigger) than most kids in his grade level and frequently beats upThe Hero? He's definitely one ofFrancis.

characters.name: Jana Tetrazini
characters.description: Early-Installment Weirdness: When her picture was first shown in "Captain Man: On Vacation", Jana was shown to be a blonde; in her actual on-screen debut in "Scream Machine", she's a brunette.

characters.name: Chapa De Silva/Volt
characters.description: Embarrassing Nickname: Hers is "Spicy Milk". Hates Wearing Dresses Red Is Heroic: She wears a red superhero suit. Shock and Awe: Her power.

characters.name: Miles Macklin/AWOL
characters.description: Embarrassing Nickname: His is "The Wing Wimp". Teleportation: His power.

characters.name: Mika Macklin/ShoutOut
characters.description: Embarrassing Nickname: Hers is "Oopsie Girl". Meaningful Name: As she puts, "Once I shout, then you're out."

characters.name: Bose O'Brian/Brainstorm
characters.description: Blue Is Heroic: He wears a blue superhero suit. Embarrassing Nickname: His is "Gorgeous Hair Boy". Mind Over Matter: His power.

characters.name: The Toddler
characters.description: Character Death: He's knocked into a bottomless pit and blown up with a bomb.Ultimately subverted, becausehe survivedinto the crossover withThe Thundermans. Depraved Dwarf: He's a dwarf who's supervillain theme is babies. Eldritch Abomination: His army of mutant babies. Hoist by His Own Petard: He says this word for word after being dropped in the bottomless pit, and blown up by his own bomb. Mister Big: SeeDepraved Dwarf.

characters.name: Dr. Horatio T. Minyak
characters.description: Captain Man's biggest arch-nemesisbesides Drex. And Then What?: Played for Laughs, when he states he will be able to do whatever he wants once he rids the city of Captain Man. His minion, Mrs. Cohort asks him what does he want to do and Minyak gives an annoyed "I don't know!" Card-Carrying Villain: When asked about why he doing what he does he claims its cause he wants to. Deadpan Snarker: "I know. I was being disrespectful." Demoted to Extra: His appearances inDanger Forceare rather minuscule in comparison to the parent series.

characters.name: Nurse Cohort
characters.description: Dr. Minyak's lowly assistant.

characters.name: Drill Finger
characters.description: An evil dentist who wears a mask. Face–Heel Turn: He was a dentist before turning to a life of crime. Karma Houdini: Due to beingThe Unfought. This Is a Drill: As per his name, he has a drill for a finger. The Unfought: Captain Man & Kid Danger don't confront him face to face at first since he was in Nebraska and Captain Man only protects Swellview.Eventually is fought in "Double Date Danger" when he comes to Swellview for malicious reasons.

characters.name: Time Jerker
characters.description: A villain who does time related crime and puns. Greater-Scope Villain: His time machine becomes the plot element for various episodes, like when Henry used it to make his bad day good only to make it his friends' bad day, when Henry used it to go back in time to find a bird declared extinct in modern times so he can cure Piper of Ox Pox and Drex used it as part of his plot to erase Captain Man from existance. Pungeon Master: Makes lots of time jokes. Time Machine: He created one. The problem with it is that it has trouble retrieving the subject and returning them to the present. Henry once used it to find a bird declared extinct in modern times so he can cure Piper of Ox Pox. It later becomes part of Drex's plot to erase Captain Man and Kid Danger from ever forming.

characters.name: Drex Stinklebaum
characters.description: A criminal who was Captain Man's original sidekick. Arch-Enemy: He is this to Captain Man and Kid Danger. Big Bad: By far the biggest and most dangerous threat in the series. Body Horror: When he ends up with Captain Man's invulnerability, his left hand tuns into that of a lizard's. Brought Down to Normal:Both him and Ray lose their indestructibility in the final episode.

characters.name: Frankini
characters.description: A social media manic who almost exposed the identities of Captain Man and Kid Danger. Ambiguously Related: It's not statedoutrightif he's Cat Valentine's weird brother, but his sociopathic behavior, the fact that Goomer used to hang out with his sister (Goomer originated fromSam & Cat, which featured Cat herself) and the fact that he's literally played by Ariana Grande's real-life brother hint that he is. Adding to this is the fact that Frankini is a musically inclined performer and Cat, a talented singer in her own right, goes to Hollywood Arts, a performing arts school. Camp Gay/Camp Straight: It isn't stated what his sexuality is, but he is definitely, absolutely camp. InHenry Danger: The Movie, he begins to say he's lived his entire life in the Gay Zone when he mishears Missy say he would hate the Gray Zone. Dirty Coward: When Captain Man performs aBig Finale Crowd Songat the end of the musical episode, Frankini attempts to talk Piper and Kid Danger out of joining the former by begging them to sing-fight him again. "Freaky Friday" Flip: In "Captain Man-kini", in order to stop a web celebrity from harming an elderly woman, Captain Man swaps bodies with Frankini.

characters.name: Jeff Bilsky
characters.description: A dumb, incompetent criminal who can never commit a crime properly. Connected All Along:"The Whole Bilsky Family" reveals he's Mitch's brother. The Ditz: There's a reason why he's called "The Stupidest Criminal in Swellview". Villain With An F in EvilHe's this in spades. So much that makes Jasper look intelligent in comparison. Nice Job Fixing It, Villain!: Jeff kidnapped Kid Danger in “Jasper Danger” along with his girlfriend, but it turns out that he actually took Jasper Dunlop and Monica instead when Captain Man answers his video call.

characters.name: Veronika
characters.description: A female vandal who's a member of the Wall Dogs. Anti-Villain: She and the other Wall Dogs see themselves as such, stating that they are only artists expressing themselves. More significant than the other Wall Dogs, as her feelings for Henry appear to be genuine. Broken Pedestal: Henry stops dating her after she is revealed to be the Mad Granny who punted Captain Man. Catchphrase: Anyone who doesn't agree with or understand the Wall Dogs' actions is referred to as a "Herbert" by her and the other members. Crocodile Tears: When Henry calls her a punk in response to herMotive Rant, she starts crying; when he tries to apologize, she quits faking and handcuffs him to a cleaning machine.

characters.name: Van Del
characters.description: The leader of the Wall Dogs.

characters.name: Rick Twitler
characters.description: The founder ofTwitFlashas well as one of the Captain Man's most cunning and intelligent foes. The Chessmaster: He was the mastermind behind the last few episodes of Season 4 and the first few episodes of Season 5, up to his official debut in "A New Evil". He had stolen the World/s Most Poisonous Cactus from Cactus Con inThe Great Cactus Conin which the gas from the Cactus is what is trapping Captain Man, and he also hacked the Man Cave while he was at Cactus Con and knows everything about him including his name, where the Man Cave is located and his age. He then tells Captain Man that he hacked the Man Cave while he was at Cactus Con and reveals that he knows everything about him including his name, where the Man Cave is located and his age. Crazy-Prepared: A side clip reveals that Rick had a backup plan in case his plan to permanently shut down the internet failed or he was killed or lost his memories. He had recorded a video of himself and made blueprints for a superweapon know as the The Omega Weapon and sent both of them to The Messenger to give to the right villain to drain Captain Man's powers and destroy him. Knight of Cerebus: When he brags about how he's not Ray's average bad guy, he's not bluffing in the slightest. He's far smarter than the average Swellview villain, and his role is neverPlayed for Laughs. He tricked the heroes into exposing themselves to toxic gases, hired a man to pose as a kidnapper to lure the heroes there, had two functioning traps prepared for them when they arrived, successfully steals Henry/Kid Danger's power, keeps Henry busy by sending thugs to his house to attack his family long enough for Rick to create a deadly virus that successfully destroys Swellview's internet, basically wins, turns into a living weapon, and even though he loses his memories in the end he's still able to get the final laugh on Henry as he's responsible for Henry losing his power and set up the mega weapon that would be used in the final arc of the show. La

characters.name: The Phone Shark
characters.description: A man who eats phones. Expy: Of Jaws of theJames Bondfilms. Scary Teeth: Has teeth of titanium so he can eat phones. Villains Out Shopping: Charlotte and Henry caught him while he was at his brother's wedding.

characters.name: Courtney Sham
characters.description: A girl revealed to have been going out with Jasper in "Jasper's Real Girlfriend". Ax-Crazy: Tries to kill Charlotte at the drop of the hat, and ends up chasing her, Henry and Jasper with what is pretty much a chainsaw. Faux Affably Evil: Sure she seems sweet at first, but then she resorts to attempted murder based on a misunderstanding. Murder the Hypotenuse: She tries to knock a cell fan on Charlotte when she gets the wrong idea that Jasper has romantic feelings for her.
